title: X-tra Creamy Korean Chicken Pajeon Banchan
description: A crispy Korean pancake banchan infused with a rich, creamy sauce, perfect for a midnight snack or side dish.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, green onions, gochugaru, and sugar.
In a separate bowl, whisk together egg and water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until a smooth batter forms.
Cut the chicken into small pieces and add to the batter, stirring until coated.
Heat 1/2 cup of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the oil and flatten slightly.
Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side or until golden brown and crispy.
Drain the pajeon on paper towels and serve hot with HP sauce and sour cream drizzled on top.
In a small bowl, whisk together HP sauce and sour cream.
Season the sauce with toasted sesame oil to taste.
Serve the pajeon with the creamy sauce spooned over the top.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
